TextView.android:text:
This app makes use of the accessibility service.
You need to grant this permission by tapping the below button which will take you to:
Settings / Accessibility / Downloaded apps.

How it works:
You have the world at your fingertips, but when you want to turn off the screen you need to hold the phone with two hands...
 LockScreen by naiveHA is here to help you
When you tap the app icon, the app launches and, soon after, navigates to this screen.
This transition is detected by the accessibility service (AccessibilityEvent.TYPE_WINDOW_STATE_CHANGE)
which then proceeds to turn off the screen.
Following this, the app moves to background such that when you unlock the screen again, the home screen is shown.

The source code of the app is open and available on github:
https://github.com/naive-HA/LockScreen

The app does not require any other permissions and does not collect any data.
The app does not access user's phone contacts nor the location.
The app does not display any ads.

This is just an app which brings the screen lcok button within the reach of your thumb
